title: Synthetic Neurons Fire - TCR 04/19/26
description:
Northwestern University engineers printed artificial neurons that successfully triggered responses in living mouse brain tissue, a federal executive order directed the FDA to accelerate review of psychedelic therapies, and Cerebras Systems filed for an IPO on $510 million in revenue with over $10 billion in combined deals from AWS and OpenAI.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-19-2026/

Printed artificial neurons made from molybdenum disulfide and graphene generated lifelike electrical signals that activated living mouse brain cells - the biological tissue responded as if the synthetic devices were native neurons. A University of Pittsburgh trial published in *Nature Communications* showed regulatory dendritic cell therapy enabled liver transplant recipients to stop immunosuppressant drugs for three years, demonstrating the body's own tolerance machinery can be deliberately engaged. A federal executive order directed the FDA to expedite psychedelic therapy review with $50 million in research funding and three priority review vouchers expected next week. Cerebras Systems filed for IPO on $510 million in 2025 revenue, with AWS and OpenAI partnerships worth over $10 billion, marking the most significant challenge to Nvidia's dominance in AI compute. The global RAM shortage - production at 60% of demand - is constraining the hardware base even as new capacity won't arrive before 2027. Lloyds Metals converted a 650-ton mining excavator from diesel to electric in-house, UK EVs crossed below petrol car prices, and JinkoSolar hit 32.76% certified efficiency on industrial perovskite-silicon tandem cells.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-19-2026/
